lesson.subtitle: Aula 16 -- Professional Emails: Structure & Tone
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Eduarda | Aula 16 -- Professional Emails

listenings.text: Hi Eduarda, quick note before you send that draft. I read the email to the client and the structure is good, but the tone is a little too direct. Please soften the opening line. Instead of give me the numbers by Friday, write I would appreciate the figures by Friday if possible. Also acknowledge their last message before you ask for anything, and keep the body concise, just three short paragraphs. Add the deck as an attachment, and copy me on the reply. Sign off with a warm regards rather than thanks. Send it to the recipient by end of day.

Hi team, here is a reminder on email tone before the deal heats up. Every message to a client should follow the same structure. Open by acknowledging what they wrote. State your purpose in one clear line, I am writing to confirm the timeline. Keep the body concise and easy to scan. If you attach a file, name it in the message so the recipient does not miss it. End with a polite sign off, and reply promptly, ideally within a few hours. A well written email protects the relationship as much as the deal itself.

extra_audio.text: Eduarda opened the email by acknowledging the client's last message. She stated her purpose in one clear line. She kept the body concise and added the report as an attachment. She copied her director on the reply. She signed off politely and sent it to the recipient.
